comment_text.sn35.245:2.1: The mendicant’s dissatisfaction perhaps stems from the term “well purified” (suvisuddha). It is not a well-defined term, but is most prominently used to describe the Buddha’s own realization of the Dhamma (SN 56.11:10.1). There, he uses a longer phrase, saying his “knowledge and vision is well purified in three rounds and twelve aspects”, denoting arahantship. The answers given here, however, speak only of the vision of the truth, which normally means stream-entry, while the abandoning of defilements characteristic of arahantship is not mentioned. Thus it seems the questioner thought the term “well purified” in and of itself implied arahantship, while his respondents took it to mean stream-entry, only implying arahantship when expanded in the longer phrase.
comment_text.sn35.245:6.1: Kiṁsuka should be resolved kiṁ-suka (“what parrot?”) rather than kiṁ-su-ka (“what’s it?”), as shown by the Sanskrit spelling kiṁśuka (Rig Veda 10.85.20; the flowers adorning the wedding chariot). In Ritusaṁhāra 6.22, a poem sometimes attributed to Kalidasa, the comparison to a parrot is made explicit. In Pali it is also referred to as palāsa, in which case I use flame-of-the-forest. It is Butea monosperma, whose distinctive flower keels resemble the curled beak of a parrot. The simile is further drawn out in the Kiṁsukopama Jātaka (Ja 248).
comment_text.sn35.245:6.3: The following are all accurate descriptions of the parrot tree.
comment_text.sn35.245:7.4: The flowers can resemble scraps of meat. Visuddhimagga 196.5–15 tells of a foolish jackal who danced in joy on finding a meat-bearing tree.
comment_text.sn35.245:7.9: The lebbeck or siris (Pali sirīsa, Albizia lebbeck) has large seed pods. It was the awakening tree of Kakusandha (DN 14:1.8.4).
comment_text.sn35.245:9.12: The Buddha’s response, by omitting a statement on the ending of defilements, affirms the earlier replies, confirming that suvisuddha by itself indicates stream-entry.

root_text.sn35.245:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:1.2: “kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.245:1.3: “Yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn35.245:2.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:2.2: “kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.245:2.3: “Yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu pañcannaṁ upādānakkhandhānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn35.245:3.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:3.2: “kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.245:3.3: “Yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu catunnaṁ mahābhūtānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn35.245:4.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:4.2: “kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.245:4.3: “Yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu yaṁ kiñci samudayadhammaṁ, sabbaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.sn35.245:5.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca: “idhāhaṁ, bhante, yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamiṁ; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.sn35.245:5.2: ‘kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī’ti?
root_text.sn35.245:5.3: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, so bhikkhu maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:5.4: ‘yato kho, āvuso, bhikkhu channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī’ti.
root_text.sn35.245:5.5: Atha khvāhaṁ, bhante, asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro bhikkhu tenupasaṅkamiṁ; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.sn35.245:5.6: ‘kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī’ti?
root_text.sn35.245:5.7: Evaṁ vutte, bhante, so bhikkhu maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn35.245:5.9: catunnaṁ mahābhūtānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe…
root_text.sn35.245:5.10: yaṁ kiñci samudayadhammaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ nirodhadhammanti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ettāvatā kho, āvuso, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī’ti.
root_text.sn35.245:5.11: Atha khvāhaṁ, bhante, asantuṭṭho tassa bhikkhussa pañhaveyyākaraṇena yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁ (…).
root_text.sn35.245:5.12: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, bhikkhuno dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hotī”ti?
root_text.sn35.245:6.2: So yenaññataro puriso kiṁsukassa dassāvī tenupasaṅkameyya. Upasaṅkamitvā taṁ purisaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:6.6: Tena kho pana, bhikkhu, samayena tādisovassa kiṁsuko yathāpi tassa purisassa dassanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:7.1: Atha kho so, bhikkhu, puriso asantuṭṭho tassa purisassa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro puriso kiṁsukassa dassāvī tenupasaṅkameyya; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ purisaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:7.5: Tena kho pana, bhikkhu, samayena tādisovassa kiṁsuko yathāpi tassa purisassa dassanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:7.6: Atha kho so bhikkhu puriso asantuṭṭho tassa purisassa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro puriso kiṁsukassa dassāvī tenupasaṅkameyya; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ purisaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:7.9: ‘ocīrakajāto kho, ambho purisa, kiṁsuko ādinnasipāṭiko—seyyathāpi sirīso’ti.
root_text.sn35.245:7.10: Tena kho pana, bhikkhu, samayena tādisovassa kiṁsuko, yathāpi tassa purisassa dassanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:7.11: Atha kho so bhikkhu puriso asantuṭṭho tassa purisassa pañhaveyyākaraṇena, yenaññataro puriso kiṁsukassa dassāvī tenupasaṅkameyya; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ purisaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:7.14: ‘bahalapattapalāso sandacchāyo kho, ambho purisa, kiṁsuko—seyyathāpi nigrodho’ti.
root_text.sn35.245:7.15: Tena kho pana, bhikkhu, samayena tādisovassa kiṁsuko, yathāpi tassa purisassa dassanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:7.16: Evameva kho, bhikkhu, yathā yathā adhimuttānaṁ tesaṁ sappurisānaṁ dassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ hoti tathā tathā kho tehi sappurisehi byākataṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:8.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhu, rañño paccantimaṁ nagaraṁ daḷhuddhāpaṁ daḷhapākāratoraṇaṁ chadvāraṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:8.2: Tatrassa dovāriko paṇḍito byatto medhāvī, aññātānaṁ nivāretā, ñātānaṁ pavesetā.
root_text.sn35.245:8.3: Puratthimāya disāya āgantvā sīghaṁ dūtayugaṁ taṁ dovārikaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:8.7: Atha kho taṁ sīghaṁ dūtayugaṁ nagarassāmikassa yathābhūtaṁ vacanaṁ niyyātetvā yathāgatamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya.
root_text.sn35.245:8.10: dakkhiṇāya disāya āgantvā sīghaṁ dūtayugaṁ taṁ dovārikaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn35.245:8.14: Atha kho taṁ sīghaṁ dūtayugaṁ nagarassāmikassa yathābhūtaṁ vacanaṁ niyyātetvā yathāgatamaggaṁ paṭipajjeyya.
root_text.sn35.245:9.1: Upamā kho myāyaṁ, bhikkhu, katā atthassa viññāpanāya.
root_text.sn35.245:9.3: ‘nagaran’ti kho, bhikkhu, imassetaṁ cātumahābhūtikassa kāyassa adhivacanaṁ mātāpettikasambhavassa odanakummāsūpacayassa aniccucchādanaparimaddanabhedanaviddhaṁsanadhammassa.
root_text.sn35.245:9.4: ‘Cha dvārā’ti kho, bhikkhu, channetaṁ ajjhattikānaṁ āyatanānaṁ adhivacanaṁ.
root_text.sn35.245:9.8: ‘Majjhe siṅghāṭako’ti kho, bhikkhu, catunnetaṁ mahābhūtānaṁ adhivacanaṁ—
root_text.sn35.245:9.11: ‘Yathāgatamaggo’ti kho, bhikkhu, ariyassetaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa adhivacanaṁ, seyyathidaṁ—

translation_text.sn35.245:0.3: The Simile of the Parrot Tree
translation_text.sn35.245:1.1: Then one mendicant went up to another mendicant and asked,
translation_text.sn35.245:1.2: “Reverend, at what point is a mendicant’s vision well purified?”
translation_text.sn35.245:1.3: “When a mendicant truly understands the origin and disappearance of the six fields of contact, at that point their vision is well purified.”
translation_text.sn35.245:2.1: Not content with that answer, that mendicant went up to a series of other mendicants and received the following answers:
translation_text.sn35.245:2.3: “When a mendicant truly understands the origin and disappearance of the five grasping aggregates, at that point their vision is well purified.”
translation_text.sn35.245:3.3: “When a mendicant truly understands the origin and disappearance of the four principal states, at that point their vision is well purified.”
translation_text.sn35.245:4.3: “When a mendicant truly understands that everything that has a beginning has an end, at that point their vision is well purified.”
translation_text.sn35.245:5.1: Not content with any of those answers, that mendicant went up to the Buddha and told him what had happened. Then he asked,
translation_text.sn35.245:5.12: “Sir, at what point is a mendicant’s vision well purified?”
translation_text.sn35.245:6.1: “Mendicant, suppose a person had never seen a parrot tree before.
translation_text.sn35.245:6.2: They’d go up to someone who had seen a parrot tree and ask them,
translation_text.sn35.245:6.3: ‘My friend, what’s a parrot tree like?’
translation_text.sn35.245:6.5: ‘A parrot tree is blackish, like a charred stump.’
translation_text.sn35.245:6.6: Now, at that time a parrot tree may well have been just as that person saw it.
translation_text.sn35.245:7.1: Not content with that answer, that person would go up to a series of other people and receive the following answers:
translation_text.sn35.245:7.4: ‘A parrot tree is reddish, like a scrap of meat.’
translation_text.sn35.245:7.9: ‘A parrot tree has flaking bark and burst pods, like a lebbeck tree.’
translation_text.sn35.245:7.14: ‘A parrot tree has luxuriant, shady foliage, like a banyan.’
translation_text.sn35.245:7.15: Now, at each of those times a parrot tree may well have been just as those people saw them.
translation_text.sn35.245:7.16: In the same way, those true persons each answered according to what they were focused on when their vision was well purified.
translation_text.sn35.245:8.1: Suppose there was a king’s frontier citadel with fortified embankments, ramparts, and arches, and six gates.
translation_text.sn35.245:8.2: And it has a gatekeeper who is astute, competent, and clever. He keeps strangers out and lets known people in.
translation_text.sn35.245:8.3: A swift pair of messengers would arrive from the east and say to the gatekeeper,
translation_text.sn35.245:8.4: ‘Worthy man, where is the lord of the city?’
translation_text.sn35.245:8.6: ‘There he is, sirs, seated at the central square.’
translation_text.sn35.245:8.7: Then that swift pair of messengers would deliver a message of truth to the lord of the city and depart the way they came.
translation_text.sn35.245:8.8: A swift pair of messengers would come from the west …
translation_text.sn35.245:8.14: deliver a message of truth to the lord of the city and depart the way they came.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.1: I’ve made up this simile to make a point.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.3: ‘City’ is a term for this body made up of the four principal states, produced by mother and father, built up from rice and porridge, liable to impermanence, to wearing away and erosion, to breaking up and destruction.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.4: ‘Six gates’ is a term for the six interior sense fields.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.6: ‘A swift pair of messengers’ is a term for serenity and discernment.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.7: ‘The lord of the city’ is a term for consciousness.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.8: ‘The central square’ is a term for the four principal states:
translation_text.sn35.245:9.9: the elements of earth, water, fire, and air.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.10: ‘A message of truth’ is a term for extinguishment.
translation_text.sn35.245:9.11: ‘The way they came’ is a term for the noble eightfold path, that is,
translation_text.sn35.245:9.12: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.”

variant_text.sn35.245:2.1: pañhaveyyākaraṇena → pañhavyākaraṇena (bj, pts1ed); pañhāveyyākaraṇena (sya-all, km, mr)
variant_text.sn35.245:8.1: daḷhuddhāpaṁ → daḷhuddāpaṁ (bj, pts1ed) | daḷhapākāratoraṇaṁ → daḷhaṁ pākāratoraṇaṁ (sya-all)
